questions.question: Which method is called on a <canvas> element to obtain its 2D drawing context?
questions.explanation: The getContext('2d') method returns a CanvasRenderingContext2D object that provides methods and properties for drawing shapes, text, and images on the canvas.

questions.question: What does the fillRect(x, y, width, height) method do on a canvas 2D context?
choices: It draws only the outline of a rectangle
It draws a filled rectangle at the specified position and size
It clears a rectangular area of the canvas
It creates a clipping region in the shape of a rectangle
questions.explanation: The fillRect method draws a rectangle filled with the current fillStyle color or pattern. Use strokeRect for outlines only, and clearRect to erase a rectangular area.

questions.question: Which canvas method is used to draw a circle or part of a circle?
questions.explanation: The arc(x, y, radius, startAngle, endAngle) method adds an arc to the current path. A full circle uses startAngle of 0 and endAngle of 2 * Math.PI.

questions.question: What is the default content displayed when a browser does not support the <canvas> element?
choices: An error message generated by the browser
The fallback content placed between the opening and closing canvas tags
questions.explanation: Any content between <canvas> and </canvas> is fallback content displayed only when the browser does not support the canvas element. Supported browsers ignore this inner content.

questions.question: The ___ property on a 2D canvas context sets the color or style used to fill shapes.
questions.explanation: The fillStyle property accepts a CSS color string, gradient, or pattern. It determines the fill color for subsequent calls to fillRect, fill, and fillText.

questions.question: The canvas method ___ draws the outline of a rectangle without filling it.
questions.explanation: The strokeRect(x, y, width, height) method draws only the border of a rectangle using the current strokeStyle and lineWidth, leaving the interior transparent.
